mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing equity valuations and producing positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed cut rates aggressively in Q4 2008
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns following month-over-month FEDFUNDS declines are negative or not significantly above zero in a 3-year rolling window (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 6.5 percent triggers a nonlinear increase in precautionary savings and a collapse in discretionary consumption that outpaces corporate cost-cutting, generating negative 60-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 6.5%, positive 60-day S&P 500 returns occur in more than 50% of triggered instances or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate declines month-over-month while the unemployment rate rises month-over-month, the magnitude of labor market deterioration exceeds the stimulative effect of monetary easing, causing negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive 20-day returns in more than 45% of triggered months, or the conditional return is not significantly lower than the unconditional return (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During deep recessions characterized by declining industrial production, a month-over-month increase in housing starts signals that residential inventory overhang is clearing and that the most severe phase of demand contraction has passed, producing positive 60-day S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:housing starts remained at historic lows in late 2008
regime_hint: deep recession with declining industrial production
failure_condition: In months where industrial production is declining, month-over-month HOUST increases are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances or the mean return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production to a new 12-month low indicates accelerating supply-side deleveraging and fixed-cost absorption by firms, generating negative 5-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its trailing 12-month minimum
evidence_refs: fact:industrial production declined sharply in late 2008
failure_condition: When INDPRO falls below its trailing 12-month minimum, positive 5-day S&P 500 returns occur in more than 50% of triggered instances or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month declines in real retail sales and industrial production confirm a synchronous contraction in final demand and manufacturing output that overwhelms inventory restocking incentives, producing negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint negative realization of RSAFS and INDPRO is followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 45% of cases, or the interaction term does not add explanatory power beyond individual series (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in nonfarm payrolls reduce aggregate wage income and precede downward corporate earnings revisions, causing negative 60-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Month-over-month PAYEMS declines are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 1 percent signals proximity to the zero lower bound, eliminating conventional easing expectations and expanding equity risk premiums, which produces negative 5-day S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed funds target was 1.0% on Dec 15 2008
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 1%, positive 5-day S&P 500 returns occur in more than 50% of triggered weeks or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).
